2016.layout_note: Table 19 collapsed-only; no move-type; poverty marginal

2017.layout_note: Table 19 collapsed-only; no move-type; poverty marginal

2022.layout_note: mig_13 single; poverty & move-type SEPARATE marginals

2023.layout_note: mig_13 single; poverty & move-type SEPARATE marginals

years_failed.2020: No CPS ASEC 1-year geographic mobility file published (COVID-19 data collection disruption); only 5-year ACS-style tables exist (different universe) - skipped per instructions.
years_failed.2024: Not yet published as of access date (no cps-2024 directory under geographic-mobility).

layout_assumptions: Columns are reason categories; rows are demographic characteristics. Poverty Status and Type of Move are ROW blocks.
2016/2017 (Table 19): collapsed reasons only (no specific sub-reasons); poverty present; NO type-of-move => move_type='all'.

2018/2019 (Table 17, 4 parts) & 2021 (mig_17, 4 parts): each part = one move type (all/within_county/different_county/from_abroad); poverty rows inside each part => genuine move_type x poverty cross-tab.

2022/2023 (mig_13 single): poverty and type-of-move are SEPARATE marginal row blocks, NOT crossed. Captured as move_type x poverty='all' and poverty x move_type='all'. No joint cross-tab available in source.

move_type='different_county' corresponds to source 'Intercounty' (different county, includes different state). Source does not split intercounty into same-state vs different-state in the reason table; 'different_state' is therefore NOT separately available.
'Intercounty or From Abroad' rollup row in single-file tables is skipped to avoid double counting.
Suppressed cells ('D','S','-') are dropped (no row emitted), not imputed.
Specific housing sub-reasons captured: own_home_not_rent, newer_better_larger_home, better_neighborhood, cheaper_housing, foreclosure_eviction, other_housing_reason.
2021 renamed 'wanted cheaper housing' -> 'cheaper housing' (both map to cheaper_housing).
